response: The global food system faces immense pressure as the world population is projected to reach 9.7 billion by 2050, necessitating a 60% increase in food production. This challenge is compounded by climate change, water scarcity, and soil degradation, which has already reduced the productivity of 33% of the world's arable land. Furthermore, agriculture contributes roughly 25% of greenhouse gas emissions, while one-third of all food produced is lost or wasted. To address these issues, various technological and policy-driven solutions are being explored, though the primary challenge remains scaling these innovations while maintaining food affordability and accessibility.
